description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by three wide, dark metal hoops. The wood has a natural, medium-brown finish with visible grain, giving it a rustic and weathered appearance. The top is flat and appears to be made of the same wood as the sides, with a small wooden bung or plug visible on one side of the lid. The metal hoops are dark, possibly iron, and show some mottling and wear, suggesting age or frequent use. On one side, the hoops are fastened with large, round metal rivets, adding to the structural integrity and visual detail. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban environments such as alleys, courtyards, or storage areas, often used for storing liquids or dry goods.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with dark metal hoops, a flat wooden top, and a small wooden bung. The surface shows rustic wood grain and aged metal details.
description_view_0: The barrel is seen with the wooden bung visible on the top edge and the metal hoops clearly wrapping around the body.
description_view_1: This view shows the flat wooden top without the bung in sight, emphasizing the circular shape and the even spacing of the metal hoops.
description_view_2: The wooden bung is again visible, but from a different angle, and the wood grain is prominent on the staves.
description_view_3: This side highlights the three metal hoops with large, round rivets visible, adding structural and visual detail.
